speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Have you perused the letters from the Pope,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The Emperor, and the Earl of Armagnac?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I have, my lord, and their intent is this:

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: They humbly sue unto your Excellence

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: To have a godly peace concluded of

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Between the realms of England and of France.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: How doth your Grace affect their motion?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Well, my good lord, and as the only means

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: To stop effusion of our Christian blood

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And stablish quietness on every side.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Ay, marry, uncle, for I always thought

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: It was both impious and unnatural

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That such immanity and bloody strife

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Should reign among professors of one faith.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Besides, my lord, the sooner to effect

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And surer bind this knot of amity,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: The Earl of Armagnac, near knit to Charles,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: A man of great authority in France,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Proffers his only daughter to your Grace

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: In marriage, with a large and sumptuous dowry.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Marriage, uncle? Alas, my years are young;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And fitter is my study and my books

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Than wanton dalliance with a paramour.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Yet call th’ Ambassadors and, as you please,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: So let them have their answers every one.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I shall be well content with any choice

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Tends to God’s glory and my country’s weal.

speaker: EXETER
text: What, is my Lord of Winchester installed

speaker: EXETER
text: And called unto a cardinal’s degree?

speaker: EXETER
text: Then I perceive that will be verified

speaker: EXETER
text: Henry the Fifth did sometime prophesy:

speaker: EXETER
text: “If once he come to be a cardinal,

speaker: EXETER
text: He’ll make his cap coequal with the crown.”

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My Lords Ambassadors, your several suits

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Have been considered and debated on;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Your purpose is both good and reasonable,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And therefore are we certainly resolved

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To draw conditions of a friendly peace,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Which by my Lord of Winchester we mean

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Shall be transported presently to France.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And for the proffer of my lord your master,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I have informed his Highness so at large

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: As, liking of the lady’s virtuous gifts,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Her beauty, and the value of her dower,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: He doth intend she shall be England’s queen.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: In argument and proof of which contract,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Bear her this jewel, pledge of my affection.—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And so, my Lord Protector, see them guarded

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And safely brought to Dover, where, inshipped,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Commit them to the fortune of the sea.

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Stay, my Lord Legate; you shall first receive

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: The sum of money which I promisèd

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Should be delivered to his Holiness

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: For clothing me in these grave ornaments.

speaker: LEGATE
text: I will attend upon your Lordship’s leisure.

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Now Winchester will not submit, I trow,

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Or be inferior to the proudest peer.

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Humphrey of Gloucester, thou shalt well perceive

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: That neither in birth or for authority

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: The Bishop will be overborne by thee.

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: I’ll either make thee stoop and bend thy knee,

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Or sack this country with a mutiny.
